steps.title: Find the slope
steps.content:
From the table, when x = -3, f(x) = 0; when x = 0, f(x) = -15.
Compute the slope: $$m = \frac{-15 - 0}{0 - (-3)} = \frac{-15}{3} = -5$$

steps.title: Write the equation using point-slope
steps.content:
Using point-slope form with point (−3,0): $$y - 0 = -5(x - (-3))$$
Simplify: $$y = -5(x + 3)$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
